title: Course Withdrawal Policy
text:
Students may drop a course without academic penalty before the DNE (Did Not Enter) deadline, which is typically the second week of the term. Dropping before this deadline removes the course from the transcript entirely.

After the DNE deadline but before the withdrawal (DISC) deadline, students may withdraw from a course. A 'DISC' notation appears on the transcript but does not affect the GPA. Withdrawing after the DISC deadline is generally not permitted except in documented exceptional circumstances, which require approval from the academic advising office.

title: Adding a Course After the Deadline
text: Late course additions are only possible during the add/drop period, which ends at the start of the second week of classes. After this period, adding a course requires written permission from both the instructor and the department, submitted to the registrar. Approval is not guaranteed and depends on class capacity and the student's academic standing.

title: GPA Calculation
text: The grade point average (GPA) is computed as the sum of (grade points x credits) for all graded courses, divided by the total number of credits attempted. Courses with a DISC, PASS, or audit notation are excluded from the GPA calculation. Repeated courses: only the most recent attempt counts toward the GPA, but all attempts remain on the transcript.

title: Academic Standing and Probation
text: A student in good standing has a cumulative GPA at or above 2.00. A GPA between 1.50 and 1.99 places a student on academic probation, during which they must meet with an advisor and may face a reduced course load. A cumulative GPA below 1.50 may result in academic suspension. Students on probation who raise their GPA above 2.00 return to good standing.

title: Tuition Refund Schedule
text: A full tuition refund is available for courses dropped before the DNE deadline. After the DNE deadline, refunds are pro-rated based on the withdrawal date according to the published refund schedule. No refund is issued for withdrawals after the 60 percent point of the term. International student fees follow a separate schedule available from the international students office.
